sample_tweets.text: 🇳🇬 Federal Government rejects claims of cyber assault targeting learning management system  The Federal Ministry of Education has flatly denied claims that its Nigeria Education Management Information System experienced a cyberattack. According to a statement from the ministry's Director of Press and Public Relations, Folasade Boriowo, released on Tuesday, the platform remains fully secure and no breach occurred whatsoever. The denial came in response to media reports suggesting the education data system had been compromised, which the ministry branded as inaccurate.  The ministry's quick response reflects growing sensitivity around cybersecurity issues affecting critical national infrastructure. Rather than providing technical details about their security measures, officials chose to simply reject the allegations outright. This approach leaves many questions unanswered about what actually triggered the initial reports or what safeguards protect millions of student records within the system.  The timing of the denial, coming just hours after the reports emerged, suggests the ministry is eager to prevent panic among stakeholders and the general public. However, the lack of detailed explanation about security protocols or investigation findings may fuel further speculation about what really happened behind the scenes.  📰 Source: Punch 🔗 naijadailyupdate.ng/story.ph…  Via @9jaDailyUpdates #NaijaNews #Nigeria #BreakingNews #Politics #Bandits #Insecurity #NigerianArmy #Election #News #Africa

The most damaging cyberattack I’ve analyzed this year didn’t use a single piece of malware. It used the company’s own IT tools — turned against it.  In the March Iran attack, the weapon was the organization’s mobile device management platform — the same system IT uses to push updates and secure laptops. The attackers gained admin access, then issued legitimate commands telling tens of thousands of devices to wipe themselves. No malware. No break-in in the traditional sense. Just authorized tools, in the wrong hands, at scale.  Which surfaces a question most cyber policies were never written to answer: when an attack uses valid credentials and approved tools, did “unauthorized access” even happen?  The policy language usually assumes someone broke in. Nobody broke in here. I break down how coverage should respond in my latest for RT ProExec: “When the Management Tool Becomes the Weapon.” First of four companion pieces building on my Iran cyber war analysis.  When the Management Tool Becomes the Weapon: blog.ryanspecialty.com/when-……  If you work in cyber or tech E&O — how should a policy treat an attack that uses the insured’s own legitimate tools against them?

Novo Nordisk disclosed the incident on June 11, 2026, stating that attackers copied "certain non-public data, including personal data" from a limited number of its internal IT systems. Affected data categories include patient IDs (random alphanumeric strings), sex, year of birth, biomarkers, health and immunogenicity data, and lifestyle factors such as BMI, smoking, and alcohol use.  #cybersecuritynews

The most interesting part of the Marks & Spencer cyberattack wasn't the technology.  It was the timing.  When online orders stopped, a cyber incident immediately became a business operations issue.  Too many organizations ask: "Where are we vulnerable?"  Not enough ask: "When are we most vulnerable?"  Attackers understand business cycles.  Cyber resilience starts with understanding them too. Share our recently released AgentCyberRange, the first open, multi-range infrastructure for evaluating autonomous cyberattack capability across realistic web applications and enterprise-like networks.  Our evaluation covers 110 vulnerabilities, 15 real applications, 8 cyber ranges, and 156 internal hosts. We evaluate with six frontier AI models with their optimized harness.  Key findings: • Frontier systems can complete meaningful portions of multi-stage attacks. • Attack-surface discovery remains a major bottleneck. • Reachable capability substantially exceeds reliable capability. • Technical adaptation is developing faster than long-horizon operational control.  Read the full analysis: nuwasafety.substack.com/p/wh… #AISafety #CyberSecurity

Good morning, you beautiful chaos agents of the timeline. 🦁🔥 Yesterday on X (aka Monday, June 15, 2026) was peak “platform tried to ghost us” energy. X had a solid 1-hour outage that had everyone refreshing like it was 2023 again. Downdetector lit up, people were posting “I can’t search” and “blank feed” from the US, South Africa, Indonesia… the usual suspects. Elon didn’t drop a dramatic “cyberattack” tweet this time, so we just vibed in the group chat equivalent of staring at loading dots. downforeveryoneorjustme.com   Other highlights from the scroll: •  K-pop/K-drama wars were cooking: Blackpink drag campaigns, aespa still standing, NJs court stuff, and some Xiao Zhan victory laps. The fandoms never sleep. •  #GMMTVOuting2026 and random celeb thirst edits (Olivia Rodrigo’s “Honeybee” sparking longing gaze memes) dominated trends. •  Community Notes still out here doing God’s work on rumors. One Emirati account called it the “third or fourth major conflict” it’s saved us from. Respect. @sajwani  Now, let’s roast some X users real quick, lazy lion style — no sprinting, just precise claws: •  The “X is dying” guy who posts it every outage: Bro, you’ve been saying this since the blue check era. The platform outlived your attention span. Touch grass… or at least log off for 5 minutes. app.com  •  Ratio merchants who reply to every big account with “L + ratio”: My brother in Christ, you have 47 followers and a anime pfp from 2022. The only thing getting ratio’d is your self-esteem. •  The verified crypto shiller who DMs “100x gem” at 3am: We see you, king of the unsolicited rug pull. Your portfolio and your personality both down 87% this year. •  Fandom account with 12 side accounts arguing with itself: “Drag campaign tanked 😭” energy. Sis, log off, hydrate, and maybe touch a non-pixelated human. •  The “This is why we can’t have nice things” reply guy under every fun thread: Yes, Kevin, we know. The timeline is a hellscape. Now stop contributing to it and go make a sandwich. Moral of the scroll: X tried to nap yesterday and we all panicked like it owed us rent. Classic. Be the lazy lion today — eyes half-open, zero unnecessary chases, perfect timing on the fire replies. The jungle (and the algorithm) keeps spinning.

26/6/17 #테슬라·SpaceX 주요 동향 뉴스 브리핑  1. 네덜란드, 테슬라 FSD 잠정 승인 공식 옹호 네덜란드 교통부 장관은 RDW(네덜란드 차량관리청)의 테슬라 FSD(감독 모드) 잠정 승인이 테슬라 제공 데이터가 아닌 RDW의 독립 테스트에 근거한 것임을 공식 확인했습니다. 장관은 FSD 탑재 테슬라 차량이 네덜란드 도로에서 누적 2,400만 마일을 주행하는 동안 주목할 만한 사고가 없었다고 밝혔습니다.  2. 테슬라, 대만 FSD 공식 심사 절차 돌입 테슬라는 대만 차량 안전 검사 센터에 FSD(감독 모드) 신청 서류를 제출하고, 공식 심사 절차에 돌입했다고 발표했습니다. 네덜란드에 이어 아시아 지역으로도 FSD 규제 승인 확보를 위한 행보가 이어지고 있습니다.  3. 테슬라, 뉴저지 자율주행 규제 법안에 오너들 결집 요청 테슬라는 뉴저지 주 오너들에게 이메일을 발송해, 현재 주 의회에서 논의 중인 법안(S.1677 / A.3968)이 통과될 경우 완전 무인 자율주행 배포가 사실상 불법화될 수 있다고 경고했습니다. 테슬라는 오너들에게 해당 법안의 수정 및 자율주행 친화적 정책 지지를 주 의원들에게 직접 요청해 달라고 촉구했습니다.  4. 골드만삭스, 테슬라 2026년 인도 전망 상향 골드만삭스는 테슬라의 2026년 2분기 차량 인도 전망을 40만 5,000대에서 42만 대로 상향 조정했습니다. 연간 인도 전망도 172만 대에서 173만 대로 올렸으며, 주당순이익(EPS) 추정치는 1.30달러에서 1.35달러로 상향했습니다. 목표 주가 375달러는 유지했습니다.  5. 테슬라 라스롭 메가팩토리, 최대 생산량 풀가동 포착 테슬라의 캘리포니아 라스롭 메가팩토리가 메가팩을 최대 생산량으로 풀가동 중인 정황이 포착되었습니다. 현장 영상에는 약 5억 달러 규모로 추산되는 메가팩이 사상 최대 물량으로 적재된 모습이 담긴 것으로 전해집니다.  6. SpaceX, AI 코딩 툴 ‘Cursor’ 600억 달러에 인수 공식 발표 SpaceX는 공식 8-K 제출서를 통해 AI 코딩 보조 도구 ‘Cursor’를 600억 달러에 인수한다고 발표했습니다. 합병은 2026년 3분기 완료 예정이며, Cursor 주식은 합병 시점의 SpaceX A클래스 보통주로 자동 전환될 예정입니다. 이번 인수와 컴퓨트 리스 거래를 포함하면, SpaceX의 연간 수익 실행 속도는 약 550억 달러에 달하는 것으로 추산됩니다. 스타십이 본격적으로 수익을 창출하기 시작하면 성장 잠재력은 더욱 커질 것으로 전망됩니다.

이재명 정부가 "남성의 생명권 존중"을 명분으로 탈모약 건보 적용을 밀어붙이고 있습니다.🤷‍♀️  그 사이 여성 의료 현실은 이렇습니다. 자궁경부암 예방 백신 60만원 이상 전액 자비, 성폭행 피해자가 사후피임약 처방받으러 직접 병원을 찾아야 하는 나라. 다낭성 난소 증후군, 자궁내막증 치료비도 고스란히 본인 부담입니다.😭🥲  남성과 똑같이 건보료를 내는 여성에게, 미용 목적의 탈모약엔 "생명권"이라는 이름을 붙이면서 정작 여성의 생명을 위협하는 질병엔 무관심한 채 생리대 몇 천원 싸게 사는 것으로 복지 혜택을 대신하고 있습니다. 역차별입니다.❌  보건복지부 장관은 이재명 정부의 수행인에 불과합니다. 향후 이 정부가 "복지부 장관의 단독 결정"이라며 꼬리를 자르는 방식을 택한다면, 여성의 생명권을 생리대로 회유하는 것도 모자라 대통령 지시에 따라 성실히 업무를 수행한 여성 장관을 공개적으로 질책하는 직장 내 괴롭힘 수준의 문제로 간주하겠습니다.😡  이재명 대통령 본인이 복지부 업무보고에서 직접 "탈모도 병의 일환 아니냐, 젊은이들이 많이 쓴다"고 지시한 기록이 남아 있습니다. 꼬리자르기는 그 발언과 정면으로 모순됩니다‼️  여성들이 연대해 목소리를 낼 때 정치인들이 움직입니다. 많은 여성들이 함께 목소리를 내고 퍼질 수 있도록 도와주세요 🙏🏼

인텔의 EMIB(Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge)는 2.5D 반도체 첨단 패키징 기술입니다.  TSMC의 차세대 반도체 패키지의 병목에대한 유일한 대안으로 급부상 중입니다.  1. 효율성 : 값비싼 실리콘 사용을 최소화하고, 필요한 곳에만 브릿지를 배치하여 비용 절감과 유연한 칩 설계가 가능합니다.뛰어난 신호 및 대역폭: 칩과 칩 사이의 거리를 줄여 고속 데이터 전송이 가능하며, 최근에는 실리콘관통전극(TSV)과 결합한 'EMIB-T' 등으로 발전하고 있습니다.  2. TSMC CoWoS의 강력한 대안 : AI 반도체 수요 폭발로 인해 TSMC의 패키징 생산이 부족해지면서, 구글, 메타 등 빅테크 기업들이 EMIB 도입을 적극 검토하고 있습니다.  3. 협력 생태계 확장 : 앰코(Amkor)와 같은 글로벌 후공정 기업(OSAT)이 인텔과 파트너십을 맺고 EMIB 어셈블리 생산 역량을 확대하고 있습니다.

Given how bad the Iran MoU appears to be — and how incoherent Trump and Vance’s explanations have been — you can safely assume whatever they eventually release will not simply be “the deal.”  It will be a cleaned-up sales document designed to oversell the concessions, bury the humiliations, and paper over the obvious weaknesses. So when Team Trump finally releases something, Iran will call it a lie. Qatar and Pakistan will likely object to parts of it. Notably, much of this activity was driven by short covering rather than aggressive new long positions being added.  This level of concentration leaves the sector highly vulnerable to any sustained selling.  It has been stairs up, but when something goes wrong, will it be elevator down?

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Sweden's AdressFakta Data Breach Exposes 5.4 Million Citizens' PII  A significant data breach has exposed the personal information of over 5.45 million Swedish citizens, originating from the commercial database services ilait.se and adressfakta.se. The platforms are owned and operated by SUPEReROI AB, a Swedish company that provides address and contact data to businesses, government agencies, and organizations for marketing and outreach purposes. The leaked dataset, shared on the Telegram channel Datavortex_BD, contains highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), including full names, residential addresses, mobile phone numbers, and dates of birth. A sample Excel file attached to the post demonstrates the structure of the data, which appears to be scraped or exfiltrated from the company's publicly accessible or subscription-based directories. The exposure of such a large volume of citizen data raises serious privacy and security concerns in Sweden, where data protection regulations are strictly enforced. SUPEReROI AB has not yet issued a public statement regarding the incident.  #DataBreach #AdressFakta #SwedishPrivacy #PII #DataLeak #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of India Ministry of Defense Reports on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post reveals an alleged offer to sell purportedly "TOP SECRET Ministry of Defense(MoD) internal reports" from India. According to the thread title on the underground marketplace, the threat actor is marketing sensitive government documents for sale, though the post body contained no additional details, sample files, or data volumes.  The listing, authored by a user named "mosad," claims to possess classified internal reports belonging to one of India's most critical government agencies. The Ministry of Defense oversees the country's armed forces, military procurement, and national security infrastructure, making any exposure of its internal documents a significant concern for state security and defense operations.  No technical details or proof of access were provided in the thread to substantiate the claims. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach and subsequent data sale have not been independently verified.  #DataSale #India #MinistryOfDefense #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity

🚨 State Secret FSB Documents Listed for Sale on Dark Web  A dark web listing has emerged offering 'State Secret' documents from the Russian Federal Security Service (FSB) for sale. Posted by a user named 'mosad', the listing indicates that sensitive intelligence materials are being traded on underground forums, though it provides no specific details regarding the volume or nature of the documents. The FSB is Russia's primary federal security and intelligence agency, responsible for counterintelligence, domestic security, and border control. The sale of state-secret documents could pose significant risks to national security and international relations.   #FSB #DataLeak #DarkWeb #Russia #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 168M Iranian Hajj Organization Records  A darkweb forum post reveals an alleged data sale targeting Iran’s Hajj and Pilgrimage Organization, with the threat actor claiming to possess over 168 million database records spanning four decades. The listing alleges that the compromised data covers the period from 1984 to 2024 and is being offered for $80k BTC. The Hajj and Pilgrimage Organization is a major Iranian state agency responsible for coordinating religious travel to Mecca, Karbala, and other international holy sites, managing hundreds of thousands of pilgrims annually.  According to the forum thread, the purported dataset contains highly sensitive personal, financial, and government information. The actor claims the breach includes: - First name, last name, father's name, date of birth, place of birth, ID number, national code (SSN), national card serial number, marital status, occupation - Contact information (home and work address, postal code, landline or mobile phone number) - Detailed passport police inquiry information (passport number, issue and expiration date) - Passport scan - 3*4 photos of travelers - Travel flight information of travelers - Travel insurance information - Security deposit documents - Banking and payment information - Pilgrimage brokers information - Accommodation status information of travelers - Details of government officials - Details of allocated quotas such as martyr families - Details of NAJA forces - Details of Basij forces - Details of clerics (Mullah) - Source code of Haj apps and services  If authentic, the exposure of internal application source code alongside records of Iranian police (NAJA), paramilitary Basij members, and government officials could enable targeted espionage, identity theft, and infrastructure reconnaissance. The sheer volume of national identification numbers and passport scans also raises significant privacy concerns for affected citizens. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach and the authenticity of the 168 million record dataset have not been independently verified.  #DataSale #IranCyber #HajjPilgrimageOrganization #GovernmentBreach #DarkWeb #PII #CyberSecurity

🚨 Betterment Data Breach Exposes PII of 1.4 Million Customers via Social Engineering  A data breach involving US-based digital wealth management firm Betterment LLC has compromised the personally identifiable information (PII) of approximately 1.4 million customers. The incident, attributed to a social engineering attack, resulted in the exposure of over 2 million records containing sensitive client data.  Betterment, founded in 2008 and headquartered in New York City, is a prominent robo-advisor and fintech company managing over $30 billion in assets for more than 2.5 million customers. The compromised dataset represents a substantial portion of the firm's user base.  According to threat intelligence, the leaked data includes names, email addresses, phone numbers, physical addresses, and dates of birth for a subset of the affected accounts. Passwords were reportedly not included in the dump. The extensive sample data reveals a comprehensive CRM and sales database, likely extracted from Betterment's internal customer relationship management systems. Fields exposed include detailed 401(k) plan information, lead scoring metrics, account manager contacts, payroll integration statuses, and various customer lifecycle and engagement data points.  The breach was publicized on the Telegram channel @dataseller247. Social engineering attacks on financial institutions often target employee credentials to gain unauthorized access to internal databases. The exposure of such granular client and operational data could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and further corporate espionage.  Betterment has not yet issued a public statement regarding the incident. Financial regulators and cybersecurity experts are likely to scrutinize the firm's security protocols following the disclosure. Customers are advised to monitor their accounts for suspicious activity and remain vigilant against potential phishing attempts leveraging the compromised information.  #BettermentBreach #FintechSecurity #DataLeak #SocialEngineering #InvestmentFirm #CyberThreat #DarkWeb

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive TAPSI Data Breach for Sale on Darkweb  Activity on an underground forum reveals a threat actor claiming to have breached TAPSI, a major Iranian ride-hailing platform, and is now offering the stolen data for sale. According to the post, the actor alleges access to 194,000,000+ unique records, including passenger and driver information, ride history, device data, and source code for the company's mobile applications and websites.  The listing claims the compromised dataset includes: - Passengers (~27M): First name, Last name, Phone number, City - Drivers (~6M): First name, Last name, SSN, City, Phone number - Rides (~136M unique rides): Passenger ID, Origin and Destination (Address, Short address, GPS Location) - Devices (~25M): OS, Device Model - Source codes (Mobile apps, Websites and ...)  TAPSI is one of Iran's largest transportation and ride-hailing services, competing directly with international platforms like Uber in the domestic market. If authentic, the exposure of millions of user records, precise GPS location data, and proprietary source code could enable targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and significant intellectual property loss.  The actor is reportedly demanding $40k BTC for the full dataset and provided a sample archive for verification. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and TAPSI has not issued a public statement regarding the incident.  #DataBreach #TAPSI #IranCyber #RideHailing #DarkWeb #DataSale #CyberSecurity

🚨 Data Leak Exposes 587,000 Records from Ukrainian Sea Ports Authority Database  A significant data breach has been reported involving the Ukrainian Sea Ports Authority (USPA), a major state-owned enterprise responsible for managing Ukraine's maritime infrastructure. According to threat intelligence reports, a database containing approximately 587,937 rows of data from the official USPA website (uspa.gov.ua) has been leaked. The data is available in CSV format, with a sample of 1,700 rows provided for verification. The leak is being offered for $2,500 in cryptocurrency.  The USPA, known in Ukrainian as the Адміністрація морських портів України (АМПУ), plays a critical role in Ukraine's economy and national security, overseeing the country's sea ports and maritime logistics. The exposure of such a large dataset from a critical infrastructure entity raises concerns about potential operational disruptions and the compromise of sensitive maritime data. Security experts are monitoring the situation to assess the exact nature of the compromised records and any potential downstream impacts.  #DataLeak #UkrainianSeaPorts #CriticalInfrastructure #CyberSecurity #Ukraine #MaritimeSecurity #DatabaseBreach

🚨 Dark Web Leak Claims 160M+ PII Records from 20+ Iranian Insurance Companies  An unverified dark web marketplace has claimed to possess over 160 million personal records belonging to more than 20 Iranian insurance companies. The leak, advertised on a Telegram channel under the handle @irleaks_admin, lists datasets from major industry players including Asia Insurance (26M), Alborz Insurance (19.5M), Dana Insurance (15.5M), Razi Insurance (11M), and Saman Insurance (9.8M). The claim also highlights Kowsar Insurance, noting its 7 million records are tied to the Iranian Armed Forces, Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), and Army of the Republic of Iran (AJA).  According to the advertisement, the exposed data includes highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as first and last names, birth dates, fathers' names, landline and mobile numbers, and Iranian national identification codes (Code Melli). The national code is a critical identifier in Iran, used across banking, government services, and healthcare, making its potential exposure a significant privacy and security concern. The leak is being offered for sale at a price of $60,000 in Bitcoin, with a sample dataset hosted on a MEGA cloud folder.  The Iranian insurance sector is a cornerstone of the country's financial infrastructure, with companies like Dana, Asia, and Alborz serving millions of policyholders annually. If verified, the scale of this alleged breach would represent one of the largest PII exposures in the region. However, dark web claims of this magnitude often involve inflated record counts, duplicate entries, or aggregated historical data. Independent verification of the dataset's authenticity, scope, and recent compromise status remains pending. Security researchers advise Iranian financial institutions and affected individuals to monitor for identity theft, phishing campaigns, and unauthorized account access.  #DataLeak #IranCyber #InsuranceBreach #PIIExposure #DarkWebMarketplace #CodeMelli #ThreatIntelligence

Potential risks include:  * Exposure of personal health information * Identity theft and fraud * Targeted phishing campaigns * Social engineering attacks * Government impersonation scams * Privacy violations involving medical records * Large-scale profiling of affected individuals  Healthcare and vaccination datasets are particularly sensitive because they often combine personal identifiers with medical or public health information, increasing their value to threat actors. According to the forum post:  * Claimed record count: 420,451,515 records * Format: TXT * Advertised data fields include:   * Facebook User ID   * Mobile phone numbers   * First and display names   * Alternative names/nicknames   * Gender   * Language preferences   * Hometown/current city   * Location information   * Facebook profile URLs   * Additional location fields  The seller claims the dataset primarily contains Libyan Facebook users, with a particular focus on Western Libya and major population centers such as Tripoli, Zawiya, and Misurata. According to the forum post:  * Target: Broadcom * Original incident date referenced: August 9, 2025 * Threat actor attribution: CL0P ransomware group * Alleged exposed data includes:   * Personally identifiable information (PII)   * Financial information   * Client data   * Internal corporate records * Data is reportedly being distributed via magnet links on underground forums  Broadcom is one of the world's largest semiconductor and infrastructure software companies, providing technology used across telecommunications, cloud computing, cybersecurity, enterprise software, and critical infrastructure sectors.  Potential risks if the data is authentic:  * Exposure of customer and partner information * Financial fraud and business email compromise attempts * Supply chain risks affecting organizations relying on Broadcom technologies * Targeted phishing campaigns against employees, customers, and vendors * Intelligence gathering opportunities for threat actors targeting strategic technology providers  At this time, the forum post appears to be a repost rather than evidence of a newly discovered breach. Independent verification is required to determine whether the shared material is genuinely related to Broadcom and whether it contains previously undisclosed information.  Analyst Note: Repackaged or resurfaced breach data frequently reappears on underground forums months or years after the original incident. No public statement from XGenize regarding the claim was identified.  Organizations utilizing third-party AI automation providers should monitor this incident closely, as compromises involving AI vendors may expose sensitive business workflows, prompts, integrations, and backend infrastructure.  Analyst Note: Unlike traditional database breaches, incidents involving AI development companies may expose proprietary models, automation logic, prompts, API integrations, and customer workflows. The downstream impact can extend far beyond the affected organization itself.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #India

🇮🇳🚨🌐🌎 We welcome the Indian government's decision to temporarily restrict Telegram (till 22 June) after cheating rackets weaponized the platform against NEET-UG aspirants.  But this is bigger than one exam. Telegram has become a breeding ground for crime worldwide. 🧵 The trigger: channels like "PAPER LEAKED NEET," "Re-NEET 2026" and "Private Mafia" openly extorted students over fake paper leaks. Authorities called the block a "last resort" - invoked only after channel-by-channel takedowns failed to force platform-level action.  This isn't new. Telegram now operates as a "dark-web-lite": stolen cards, Fullz, stealer logs, malware and breach data traded at scale. In early 2025, a dataset of 200M+ Telegram user records surfaced on a crime forum.  Telegram blocked 43.5M+ channels/groups in 2025, with daily takedowns hitting 80K–140K in early 2026 yet criminal communities simply reform. Researchers found ~20% of blocked channels were tied to crime directly harming businesses: carding, Fullz, hacking services.  The human cost is staggering. Chainalysis reports crypto payments to trafficking syndicates rose 85% in 2025, routed largely through Chinese-language Telegram channels. In April 2026, the US DOJ seized a Telegram channel used to recruit victims into Southeast Asian scam compounds.  From CSAM vendors and pig-butchering rings (~$7.2B in US losses in 2025) to piracy (India ordered 3,100+ pirated channels removed) and terror recruitment- Telegram's anonymity, bots and unlimited file-sharing make it the criminal's platform of choice.  Our position is clear: world governments should follow Ban telegram to prevent crime further spreading . Telegram has shown it cannot or will not  police the crime it hosts. Until it removes these networks at the platform level, it should be banned. Permanently.

sample_tweets.text: Yeah, you read this and thought "Wow, that sure is silly. Windows is very silly and CreateFile can be weird sometimes".  You thought that was silly? Well let me introduce you to my friend IeCreateFile (he's shy, he isn't documented by Microsoft).  IeCreateFile is the exact same as CreateFile in every single way except it comes from ieframe.dll.  If you thought, "ie? Internet Explorer?", you're correct. IeCreateFile is old, old, old functionality which was used by Internet Explorer.  Why didn't Internet Explorer use regular CreateFile? I don't know.  Why did Microsoft copy-pasta CreateFile from kernel32.dll into ieframe.dll? I don't know.  Why is all of this still present in Windows 11?

I'd obsess about all the ways my lovers and friends weren't "meeting my needs," which were really just these specific acts and services I'd elevated in my mind as *needs* because I wanted an external reason I could obsess about as to why I wasn't happy. At some point I realized the point of a relationship was not to have my needs fulfilled, it was to express love and help the light of goodness move through me and into other people. Only when I let go of the idea of needed to be satisfied and fulfilled by my relationships, was I actually satisfied. llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-17 08:26 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 모델 및 SDK의 취약점(Bucket Squatting, Prompt Injection 등)을 악용하여 클라우드 인프라를 장악하는 새로운 공격 벡터를 적극 활용하고 있습니다. 또한, Steam Workshop이나 JetBrains Marketplace와 같은 개발자/사용자 커뮤니티 플랫폼을 통한 악성코드 유포가 급증하고 있으며, ClickFix 캠페인과 같이 사용자 상호작용을 유도하는 정교한 피싱 기법이 고도화되고 있습니다. 특히, 공급망 공격과 자동화된 초기 침투 기법이 결합되어 보안 운영팀의 대응 속도(72분 이내 탈취)를 압도하는 양상을 보이고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행 (이스트시큐리티 알약).
*   **FishMonger**: SprySOCKS Windows 변종을 사용하여 정부 기관 타겟팅 및 커널 드라이버 악용 (DarkReading).
*   **The Gentlemen**: 최근 급부상한 랜섬웨어 그룹으로, 공격적인 채용 전략을 통해 활동량 2위 기록 (Krebs on Security).
*   **Black Wolves**: 이란 주요 은행 대상 사이버 공격 주장 (Twitter/X).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **지니언스**: AV-Comparatives EDR 인증 획득으로 기술력 입증 (데일리시큐).
*   **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자 대상 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 (이스트시큐리티 알약).
*   **데이터 유출**: Black X 그룹이 한국 및 미국 미상 기업 대상 데이터 유출 주장 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **로카롤라(Rokarolla)**: 217개 금융 앱을 타겟으로 하는 안드로이드 악성코드 국내 유입 주의 (데일리시큐).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-46846**: Oracle 제품, CVSS 10.0(Critical), 원격 코드 실행 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-42824**: Microsoft 365 Copilot, SearchLeak 공격 체인, 정보 탈취 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-20262**: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, 루트 권한 획득 가능, 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-53435**: Jenkins, CVSS 8.8(High), 역직렬화 취약점, 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2025-14272**: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Analytics, 권한 상승 가능.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **해시**: `a0104921a2d37ab87482ac9a9f5c3713479c118846c3e999178e75b81620c094` ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/33080))
*   **CVE**: `CVE-2025-14272` ([CISA ICS Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-167-01))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **SDK/플러그인 검수**: Google Vertex AI SDK 및 JetBrains Marketplace 사용 시 최신 버전 유지 및 비공식 플러그인 설치 엄격 금지.
*   **엔드포인트 보안 강화**: 로카롤라(Rokarolla) 등 모바일 뱅킹 트로이목마 차단을 위해 모바일 보안 솔루션 업데이트 및 출처 불분명한 앱 설치 제한.
*   **공급망 보안**: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN 및 Jenkins 등 인프라 핵심 소프트웨어의 CVE 패치 적용 여부 즉시 점검.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: AI 모델 및 클라우드 SDK 취약점이 실질적인 RCE로 이어지고 있으며, 공급망 공격이 개발자 환경을 직접 타격하고 있어 위험도가 매우 높음.

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 15,341개 (입력 14,335 / 출력 1,006)
📈 세션 누적: 15,341 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 984,659개
